orgDesign.companyProfile: Typical mid-size B2B SaaS company with dedicated revenue, marketing, support, finance, engineering, and enterprise-data organizations. The organization is now modeled as a network of smaller teams sized around 10 FTE.
orgDesign.operatingModel: Business-domain teams own operational workflows while engineering platform and enterprise data teams provide reusable foundations and management visibility. Larger value streams are split into sibling teams so accountability stays close to concrete capability ownership.
designPrinciples: Operational workflow ownership sits with the function that is accountable for the KPI.
Cross-cutting controls and data products remain shared so the company operates on one model.
Internal product teams are measured by business leverage, not just feature throughput.
orgDesign.staffingPrinciple: Prefer teams of roughly 8-11 FTE, with broader customer streams split into multiple sibling teams inside the same group or team family.
orgDesign.groupingPrinciple: Use top-level groups for major value streams and a second layer of subgroups for team families when a stream contains multiple 10-FTE teams.
orgDesign.groupLeadershipPrinciple: When a group contains several 10-FTE teams, add lightweight group-level leadership and principal IC roles rather than scaling any single team too far beyond its local scope.
